content: AI is not a tool. AI is work. AI is, in fact, workers that can actually use tools. The computer I'm talking about here is a factory. It runs basically one thing. It runs AI. This factory produces one thing.

We have three fundamental technology skills. We have these three technology scaling laws — pretraining, post-training, and now test-time compute. The amount of thinking is making the models more intelligent. The more intelligent it is, the more people use it. The more people use it, the more computation is necessary. We have now reached a virtuous cycle.

We need a special computer designed for thinking, designed for reasoning. And that's what Blackwell is — a thinking machine. Regurgitating memorized content is easy. Regurgitating the multiplication table is easy. Thinking is hard.

NVIDIA is the only company in the world today that literally starts from a blank sheet of paper and can think about new fundamental architecture, computer architecture, new chips, new systems, new software, new model architecture, and new applications all at the same time.
Grace Blackwell per GPU is ten times the performance. The lowest cost tokens in the world are generated by Grace Blackwell NVLink 72.

The largest infrastructure buildout in human history. AI is infrastructure. Countries should treat AI like electricity or roads. We now have a new industry, an AI industry, and it's now part of the new infrastructure, called intelligence infrastructure, that will be used by every country, every society.

AI's infrastructure is a five-layer stack: Energy, Chips and Computing, Cloud Data Centers, AI Models, and Applications — where the economic benefit will happen. The buildout creates employment across multiple sectors: energy, construction, advanced manufacturing, cloud operations, and application development.
AI agents are the new digital workforce. The IT department of every company is going to be the HR department of AI agents in the future.
The transition to Agentic and Physical AI will be revolutionary, giving rise to new applications, companies, products, and services. Physical AI — AI that can perceive, reason, plan and act.
The ChatGPT moment for robotics is here. Breakthroughs in physical AI — models that understand the real world, reason, and plan actions — are unlocking entirely new applications.

I've said before that I expect data center buildout to reach a trillion dollars, and I am fairly certain we're going to reach that very soon. These AI data centers, if you will, are improperly described. They are, in fact, AI factories. You apply energy to it, and it produces something incredibly valuable, and these things are called tokens.

Thinking puts incredible amounts of computation load on the infrastructure because it's thinking on our behalf for every single human. So the amount of computation necessary for AI to think, inference, is really quite extraordinary. The number of people using inference has gone from 8 million to 800 million — 100x in just a couple of years.

We now have a new industry, an AI industry, and it's now part of the new infrastructure. Every country, every company should own its intelligence, should own its AI. Sovereign AI is critical — the data, the culture, the knowledge of a country should be expressed in its own AI.
Greatness is not intelligence. Greatness comes from character. And character isn't formed out of smart people; it's formed out of people who suffered.

Rather than destroying jobs, AI enhances worker productivity and purpose. In radiology and nursing, AI handles routine tasks while enabling professionals to focus on patient care and outcomes, paradoxically increasing demand for these roles.

2025 saw one of the largest years for VC funding on record, with over $100 billion deployed to AI-native companies. The AI industry is now producing the next generation of companies that will be among the most valuable in the world.

We have now reached a virtuous cycle of CUDA. When the installed base of CUDA is so large that every model trained on CUDA is better, every application runs faster, every developer knows CUDA — the ecosystem becomes self-reinforcing in ways that are very hard for competitors to disrupt.

We spent 20 years building CUDA before the world knew what to do with it. When something is important enough and the technology is right, you stay the course. The companies that abandoned GPU computing in 2010 because it had no market are the companies that weren't there when deep learning happened.

NVIDIA pushed back publicly on AI bubble allegations — a remarkable step for the company — citing the genuine productivity gains and ROI being generated by enterprise AI deployments. The company argued that the infrastructure buildout reflects real demand, not speculative excess.

AI is advancing at an incredible pace. We are at the iPhone moment of AI. Every industry will be transformed. The question is not whether AI will change your business — the question is whether you will be the one changing it or whether someone else will change it for you.
